choicesItalian: Sto cercando l'ultimo romanzo di Elena Ferrante.
Dov'è la stazione dei treni più vicina?
Chi ha scritto questo libro di ricette?
choicesEnglish: I'm looking for the latest novel by Elena Ferrante.
I want to buy a sports newspaper.
Where is the nearest train station?
correctAnswerItalian: Sto cercando l'ultimo romanzo di Elena Ferrante.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'm looking for the latest novel by Elena Ferrante.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una richiesta specifica tratta dal dialogo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerca il romanzo della Ferrante.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A specific request from the dialogue.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's looking for Ferrante's novel.

choicesItalian: È in cucina sopra il tavolo.
Non lo so proprio, mi spiace.
choicesEnglish: It should be in the new arrivals section.
It's in the kitchen on the table.
Under the bed in the room.
I really don't know, I'm sorry.
correctAnswerEnglish: It should be in the new arrivals section.

feedback.correctEnglish: Good! It indicates the correct section.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's among the new arrivals.

choicesItalian: È proprio lì in fondo a destra.
choicesEnglish: It's right there at the back on the right.
It's on the left near the entrance.
correctAnswerItalian: È proprio lì in fondo a destra.
correctAnswerEnglish: It's right there at the back on the right.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la posizione nel negozio.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the position in the shop.

choicesItalian: È un regalo per mio padre?
Chi sei tu e cosa vuoi?
choicesEnglish: Is the paperback edition out yet?
Is it a gift for my father?
Who are you and what do you want?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is the paperback edition out yet?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiede della versione meno cara.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! He's asking about the cheaper version.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's asking about the paperback edition.

choicesEnglish: The book is sold out everywhere.
correctAnswerEnglish: We only have the hardcover.

feedback.correctItalian: Bene! Specifica il tipo di rilegatura.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, hanno solo la copertina rigida.
feedback.correctEnglish: Good! It specifies the type of binding.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they only have the hardcover.

choicesItalian: Lo compro in un altro posto.
choicesEnglish: I'll take it anyway, thanks a lot.
I don't want it anymore, thanks.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'll take it anyway, thanks a lot.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he decides to take it.

choicesItalian: Vorrei fare un regalo a un amico.
choicesEnglish: I'd like to make a gift to a friend.
I want a lemon ice cream.
I'm looking for a pair of shoes.
I'm going to the cinema tonight.
correctAnswerItalian: Vorrei fare un regalo a un amico.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'd like to make a gift to a friend.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Spiega il motivo della ricerca.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un regalo per un amico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! It explains the reason for the search.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a gift for a friend.

choicesItalian: Il mio amico ama i gialli.
Il mio amico è un cuoco.
correctAnswerItalian: Il mio amico ama i gialli.
correctAnswerEnglish: My friend loves mysteries.

feedback.correctEnglish: Good! It specifies the favorite genre.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he loves mystery books.

choicesEnglish: What do you recommend as a read?
correctAnswerEnglish: What do you recommend as a read?

feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! He's asking for a suggestion.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's asking for advice.

choicesEnglish: I recommend the latest by Camilleri.
correctAnswerEnglish: I recommend the latest by Camilleri.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he recommends Camilleri.

choicesEnglish: Do you prefer something more modern?
Do you want to eat pasta?
Did you see the movie yesterday?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you prefer something more modern?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede se preferisce il moderno.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's asking if he prefers modern.

correctAnswerEnglish: Camilleri is always a guarantee.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! He expresses trust in the author.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he trusts Camilleri.

choicesEnglish: Does this book cost a lot?
correctAnswerEnglish: It's set in Sicily, right?

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! He's asking for confirmation on the location.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's asking about Sicily.

correctAnswerEnglish: It's another Montalbano investigation.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it mentions Montalbano.

choicesEnglish: Could you make me a gift wrap?
Can you bring me a coffee?
Will you give me a discount?
correctAnswerEnglish: Could you make me a gift wrap?

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he wants the gift wrap.

choicesItalian: Lo faccia da solo a casa.
choicesEnglish: Certainly, I'll prepare it right away.
correctAnswerEnglish: Certainly, I'll prepare it right away.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he prepares it right away.

choicesEnglish: I'm looking for the sports section.
correctAnswerEnglish: Where is the mystery section?

feedback.correctEnglish: Good! He's looking for a specific category.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's looking for mysteries.

correctAnswerEnglish: There's this Scandinavian author.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about the Scandinavian author.

choicesEnglish: It's very gripping, I assure you.
correctAnswerEnglish: It's very gripping, I assure you.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Positive comment on the book.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's gripping.

choicesEnglish: The paperback edition will be out soon.
The book is out of print.
correctAnswerEnglish: The paperback edition will be out soon.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it will be out soon.

choicesEnglish: Do you have any guides for Rome?
I want a pizza in Rome.
Where is the Colosseum in Rome?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you have any guides for Rome?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerca le guide di Roma.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Common question in a bookstore.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's looking for Rome guides.

choicesEnglish: I'm looking for an Italian dictionary.
I'm looking for an Italian friend.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'm looking for an Italian dictionary.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's looking for the dictionary.

choicesEnglish: Can you order it if it's not here?
Can you read it out loud?
Can you sell it at half price?
Can you give it to me now?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you order it if it's not here?

feedback.correctItalian: Bene! Soluzione se il libro manca.
feedback.correctEnglish: Good! Solution if the book is missing.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's asking to order it.

choicesItalian: Quanto tempo ci vuole per l'arrivo?
choicesEnglish: How long does it take to arrive?
How much does the trip cost?
Who is coming to pick me up?
Where do I have to go?
correctAnswerItalian: Quanto tempo ci vuole per l'arrivo?
correctAnswerEnglish: How long does it take to arrive?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede quanto tempo ci vuole.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! He's asking about the wait.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's asking how long it takes.

choicesItalian: Mi avvisate quando arriva il libro?
choicesEnglish: Will you let me know when the book arrives?
Will you call me for dinner?
Will you tell me where you are?
Will you send me a photo?
correctAnswerItalian: Mi avvisate quando arriva il libro?
correctAnswerEnglish: Will you let me know when the book arrives?

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he wants to be notified.

correctAnswerEnglish: The plot seems very interesting.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about the plot.

choicesItalian: C'è lo sconto per gli studenti?
correctAnswerItalian: C'è lo sconto per gli studenti?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is there a student discount?

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's asking for the student discount.

choicesEnglish: I want a book of poems.
I want a piece of bread.
I want to sleep a bit.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he wants poems.

correctAnswerEnglish: This author is very famous.

choicesEnglish: Do you have books for small children?
Do you have cats at home?

feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Looking for a specific target.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's looking for children's books.

correctAnswerEnglish: Where is the non-fiction section?

choicesEnglish: Can I pay with the debit card?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can I pay with the debit card?

choicesEnglish: Here is the receipt for you.
Here is the bread for you.
Here is the key for you.
Here is the road for you.
correctAnswerEnglish: Here is the receipt for you.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the receipt.

choicesEnglish: Do you want a paper bag?

choicesEnglish: Enjoy your reading and have a good day!

Have a good job and goodbye!
